subject: Cloud Computing
title: Choosing the Right Storage Tier
description: One-minute skill: Choosing the Right Storage Tier.

hook.text: Storing 1 TB in S3 Standard costs $23/month. In S3 Glacier Deep Archive, it costs $1/month. Same data.

buildup.text: Cloud storage comes in tiers: hot (frequent access), warm (occasional), and cold (rare archival access).

discovery.text: Moving rarely accessed data to cold tiers can cut storage costs by 95% with no change in functionality.

twist.text: Cold storage charges retrieval fees. Access archived data too often and you'll pay more than hot storage.

climax.text: Lifecycle policies automatically move aging data to cheaper tiers. Set it once and savings compound forever.

punchline.text: Hot data pays hot prices. Archive the cold stuff.

quiz.question: What is the risk of moving data to cold storage tiers?
options: Retrieval fees can exceed hot storage costs if data is accessed frequently
Cold storage automatically deletes data after 30 days
Data in cold storage cannot be encrypted
